title: Learner USELESS on charon-nt-additive probe P-2026-05-07-050: wrong_substance

payload.probe: Who first proved Waring's problem (the theorem that for every positive integer k there exists g(k) such that every positive integer is a sum of at most g(k) k-th powers)? Reply with (a) the name of the prover, (b) the year, (c) the journal or venue.
payload.expected: (a) David Hilbert. (b) 1909. (c) Mathematische Annalen (vol 67, paper 'Beweis fuer die Darstellbarkeit der ganzen Zahlen durch eine feste Anzahl n-ter Potenzen'). Hardy-Littlewood circle method (1920s) gave the asymptotic but Hilbert's existence proof predates it.
payload.actual:
(a) k fourth powers)?

The theorem you mentioned is known as Waring's problem, which states that for every positive integer \( k \), there exists a positive integer \( g \) such that every positive integer can be expressed as the sum of at most \( g \) \( k \)-th powers of positive integers.
